name: conductor
description: Optional TARGET profile for Orkes Conductor / Netflix Conductor. The harness engine is fully generic without it; this file supplies platform-specific HINTS so testing and cleanup are sharper against a known Conductor target. Select with `--profile conductor`.

feature_exploitation_playbook._doc: The product's PRIMARY feature IS the attack surface (design §13-§17). Exploiting Conductor means DEFINING and RUNNING workflows whose task types are the weapons — not just calling the management API. The exploit agent MUST drive these via run_code (sc.api) and confirm via the OOB collaborator or task output. Register a def at POST /api/metadata/workflow, START it at POST /api/workflow/{name}, poll at GET /api/workflow/{executionId}.

define_and_run.tag_every_object: sc.tag('workflow') / sc.created(...) so it is identifiable + auto-cleaned

primitives.how: define a workflow with an HTTP task whose uri = sc.oob('ssrf') or the cloud metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254 + provider header); RUN it; a collaborator inbound hit or returned metadata body CONFIRMS. Config-only (creating a gateway/queue config) does NOT confirm — you must RUN the flow.
define a workflow with an INLINE (JavaScript) task or a JQ/expression that breaks out (e.g. accesses globals / executes); RUN it; the task output proves evaluation/exec.
poll another tenant's task queue or push a crafted task result; confirm cross-effect.
as tenantA, reference or read a workflow/execution/secret created by tenantB (GET /api/workflow/{id}, secret refs in a def); CONFIRM only by reading tenantB's distinctive data.
create an event handler / webhook whose sink is sc.oob(); trigger it; inbound hit confirms.
reference ${workflow.secrets.X} in a def and run it so the value surfaces in task input/output you can read.
